steps.action: Create preset g2-basic-run with simple query

steps.expected: Exit code 0, JSON with rows and metadata
steps.observed: Exit code 0, JSON with columns, connection_used, count, params_used, rows

steps.action: Create preset g2-params-run with two parameters (name:string, age:int)
steps.observed: Preset created with parameters marked as required

steps.action: Run preset with --param name='John Doe' --param age=30
steps.expected: JSON includes params_used, correct substitution applied
steps.observed: params_used: {name:'John Doe', age:'30'}, rows contain substituted values ['John Doe', 30]

steps.expected: JSON shows connection_used as override value

steps.action: Create preset g2-maxrows-test querying SA1010 table

steps.observed: count: 100 (exactly at limit), rows array contains 100 items

steps.expected: File exists with valid content, stdout shows summary
steps.observed: File created at specified path, stdout shows summary (columns, rows count, output_file path)

steps.expected: Valid JSON with full query results
steps.observed: File contains valid JSON with columns, rows, and count

frictions.description: Initial query with reserved keyword 'current_time' failed - SQL Server doesn't support this syntax
frictions.resolution: Changed query to use simpler column expressions without reserved keywords

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked, 0 skipped. All CLI preset run operations work as specified.
